title: Laborie Portascan 3D Bladder Scanner - Probe cable or transducer failure
description: Troubleshooting Portascan 3D probe, cable, connector, image acquisition, or transducer recognition problems from external causes.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the Portascan 3D is not actively being used for a patient scan. If the scanner is needed for clinical decision-making and the probe signal is unreliable, have staff use another verified bladder scanner or alternate clinical assessment method. Expected outcome: Patient care is not delayed or based on an unreliable scan result.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Probe or Transducer Problem
steps.instructions: Ask clinical staff what was observed. Common reports may include: Probe not recognized; No scan image; Intermittent image dropout; Poor or distorted scan image; Cable must be moved for the probe to work; Scanner freezes during scan attempt; Error message related to probe, transducer, or acquisition; Physical damage to the probe head or cable. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified before troubleshooting begins.

steps.title: Inspect the Probe Head Externally
steps.instructions: Check the transducer face for cracks, dents, delamination, fluid intrusion, dried gel buildup, or impact damage. Do not use a probe with visible cracking, separation, sharp edges, or suspected fluid ingress. Expected outcome: The probe face is clean, intact, and safe to place near a patient. If damage is found, remove the device from service and stop.

steps.title: Check the Probe Cable Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect the full cable length for cuts, crushed sections, exposed conductors, kinks, pulled strain reliefs, or areas that appear flattened from bed rails or cart wheels. Gently flex the cable while observing whether the scanner recognizes the probe or whether the image drops out. Expected outcome: The cable remains stable and does not cause intermittent operation. If movement of the cable causes failure, suspect cable or probe assembly damage and remove the device from service.

steps.title: Inspect the Probe Connector
steps.instructions: Disconnect and inspect the probe connector only if safe and permitted by facility procedure. Look for bent pins, debris, moisture, corrosion, loose locking hardware, cracked connector housing, or signs of forced connection. Reconnect the probe securely and confirm the connector seats fully. Expected outcome: The connector is clean, dry, undamaged, and firmly seated. If connector damage or contamination is found, do not continue clinical use.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Scanner
steps.instructions: Turn the scanner off, wait briefly, and restart it. Confirm whether the probe is recognized during startup or before attempting a scan. Expected outcome: Temporary software or communication lockups clear after restart. If the probe is recognized and scans normally after restart, document the event and return the unit only after a successful functional check.

steps.title: Verify Battery or Power Stability
steps.instructions: Confirm the unit has adequate charge or is connected to a known-good charging/power source if applicable. Low or unstable power can sometimes appear as scan interruption, freezing, or probe communication failure. Expected outcome: The scanner has stable power during startup and scan attempt. If the device powers off, freezes, or loses probe recognition during power changes, remove it from service for bench evaluation.

steps.title: Check for Gel, Cleaning Residue, or Contamination
steps.instructions: Inspect the probe face, connector area, and cable strain relief for excess gel, dried cleaning solution, or fluid residue. Clean the external surfaces according to facility-approved cleaning procedures and allow the device to dry before retesting. Expected outcome: No residue interferes with probe contact, connector seating, or safe handling. If fluid intrusion is suspected, remove the device from service.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Functional Test
steps.instructions: Use the scanner in a safe test condition, following facility procedure, to confirm that the probe initializes, scans, and produces a reasonable image/acquisition response. Avoid relying only on one scan attempt if the original complaint was intermittent. Expected outcome: The probe functions consistently without dropout, freezing, or recognition errors. If the issue does not repeat and the device passes functional checks, document findings and return to service per facility policy.

steps.title: Compare With a Known-Good Scanner or Probe Only If Available
steps.instructions: If your facility has a compatible known-good Portascan 3D probe or scanner, compare behavior without forcing connectors or mixing incompatible accessories. This helps determine whether the fault follows the probe/cable assembly or remains with the scanner body. Expected outcome: The suspected component is isolated. If the problem follows the probe, remove the probe assembly from service. If the problem remains with the scanner, remove the scanner for bench evaluation.

steps.title: Check for Repeated Staff Reports or History
steps.instructions: Review recent work orders for similar complaints. Repeated intermittent probe complaints often indicate cable strain, connector wear, or internal probe failure even if the device briefly passes testing. Expected outcome: Historical patterns are considered before returning an unreliable scanner to clinical use.

steps.title: Do Not Attempt Internal Probe or Transducer Repair
steps.instructions: Do not open the probe, splice the cable, bypass connector faults, or attempt internal transducer repair. Bladder scanner probes are patient-contact diagnostic accessories. Internal repair attempts can affect safety, accuracy, cleaning integrity, and device reliability. Expected outcome: Troubleshooting remains within safe Clinical Engineering limits.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported the Laborie Portascan 3D intermittently failed to recognize the probe and would not complete bladder scans.
CCR.Cause: Inspection found probe operation dropped out when the cable was flexed near the strain relief, indicating a likely probe cable failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the Portascan 3D from service, labeled it Out of Service, and sent the probe/scanner assembly for repair evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Connector inspected for bent pins, moisture, or debris
Probe face inspected for cracks or gel buildup
Whether issue was intermittent or constant
Known-good scanner or probe comparison, if available
Cleaning residue or fluid intrusion suspected

title: Laborie Portascan 3D Bladder Scanner - Battery not holding charge
description: Troubleshooting Portascan 3D short battery runtime, charging failure, shutdowns, or battery indication problems from external causes.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the Portascan 3D is not being used for an active patient scan. If the unit shuts down during use or cannot remain powered long enough to complete a scan, provide clinical staff with another available bladder scanner. Expected outcome: Patient care is not delayed by a device with unreliable battery power.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Battery Problem
steps.instructions: Ask staff what they observed. Common reports may include: Battery drains quickly; Unit shuts off after charging; Battery icon does not increase; Scanner only works while connected to power; Charging indicator does not appear; Device powers off during startup or scanning. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified before replacing parts or accessories.

steps.title: Check the External Charger and Power Adapter
steps.instructions: Inspect the charger, power supply, and cord for cuts, bent pins, loose strain relief, broken connectors, or signs of overheating. Connect the charger firmly to the scanner or charging base, depending on the facility setup. Expected outcome: The charger should connect securely and show normal charging behavior. If the charger is damaged or excessively warm, remove it from service and test with a known-good compatible charger if available.

steps.title: Verify the Wall Outlet
steps.instructions: Plug the charger into a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Avoid power strips, loose wall outlets, switched outlets, or outlets controlled by bed or headwall switches. Expected outcome: The scanner begins charging normally from a verified power source. If the device charges normally from another outlet, document the original outlet concern and stop troubleshooting the scanner.

steps.title: Check the Charging Indicator
steps.instructions: Observe the Portascan 3D display or charging light while connected to power. Look for a battery icon, charging symbol, LED, or other indication that charging has started. Expected outcome: The device should indicate that external power is connected and the battery is charging. If there is no charging indication with a known-good outlet and charger, continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect Charging Contacts and Connector Area
steps.instructions: Check the scanner charging port, cradle contacts, and battery contact area if externally accessible. Look for debris, bent contacts, corrosion, fluid residue, cracked plastic, or loose fit. Do not probe aggressively or disassemble internal battery compartments beyond normal user-accessible areas. Expected outcome: Contacts and connectors are clean, dry, aligned, and secure. If contamination is present, clean only according to facility-approved cleaning practices and manufacturer guidance.

steps.title: Reseat the Scanner on the Charging Base
steps.instructions: If the Portascan 3D uses a docking or charging cradle, remove and reseat the scanner. Make sure it sits fully in the cradle and is not blocked by a cover, strap, label, or debris. Expected outcome: The charging indicator appears consistently when the scanner is docked. If charging starts after reseating, monitor briefly, document the cradle seating issue, and stop.

steps.title: Test With a Known-Good Charger or Dock
steps.instructions: If another compatible charger, power supply, or cradle is available, test the scanner with it. Do not mix chargers unless compatibility is confirmed by facility records, labeling, or manufacturer documentation. Expected outcome: The scanner should charge normally with a verified compatible charging setup. If the device charges normally with another charger or dock, remove the failed accessory from service.

steps.title: Allow a Full Charge Cycle
steps.instructions: Leave the scanner connected to verified power long enough for a normal charge cycle. After charging, power the unit on and observe whether the battery indicator shows a reasonable charge level. Expected outcome: The battery level should rise and remain stable long enough for normal clinical use. If the battery still drains quickly after a full charge, continue.

steps.title: Check for Storage or Use Pattern Issues
steps.instructions: Ask staff whether the scanner has been left off the charger, stored unplugged, or used repeatedly without being returned to the charging base. Battery complaints are sometimes caused by workflow rather than equipment failure. Expected outcome: The scanner’s charging routine is confirmed. If the issue is related to charging habits, educate staff and document the finding.

steps.title: Observe Runtime Under Normal Use
steps.instructions: With the battery charged, power on the scanner and perform a basic functional check using normal startup and scan preparation screens. Do not use an active patient for testing unless clinical staff require it and it is safe. Expected outcome: The scanner remains powered without sudden shutdown, rapid battery drop, or low-battery alarm. If the scanner shuts down quickly or the battery drops abnormally fast, suspect battery deterioration or internal charging circuitry.

steps.title: Check for Heat, Smell, or Physical Battery Concerns
steps.instructions: Inspect the scanner and charger for unusual heat, swelling, odor, discoloration, or melted plastic. Expected outcome: No abnormal heat, smell, swelling, or damage is present. If any of these are found, stop troubleshooting immediately, remove the device from service, and escalate for repair evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Laborie Portascan 3D battery would not hold charge and the scanner powered off shortly after removal from the charger.
CCR.Cause: Verified outlet and charger operation, but the scanner battery level dropped rapidly after a full charge cycle.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the Portascan 3D from service, labeled it Out of Service, and sent it for battery and charging system evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Battery level before and after charging
Unusual heat, smell, swelling, or discoloration

title: Laborie Portascan 3D Bladder Scanner - Thermal printer failure
description: Troubleshooting Portascan 3D printer problems caused by paper, power, settings, cover position, or external printer faults.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the bladder scan exam is complete and the device is not actively being used for patient assessment. Do not delay patient care to troubleshoot a printer issue. If printed results are needed immediately, have clinical staff document the measurement manually or use another available scanner. Expected outcome: Patient care continues while the printer issue is evaluated safely.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Printer Failure
steps.instructions: Ask staff what happened when they attempted to print. Common reports may include: No printout; Blank paper feeds; Paper jam; Paper will not advance; Printer cover will not latch; Print is faint or incomplete; Printer makes noise but does not print; Device displays a printer or paper-related message. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified before parts or accessories are blamed.

steps.title: Check Device Power and Startup Condition
steps.instructions: Verify the Portascan 3D powers on normally and completes startup without unrelated system errors. If the scanner itself is unstable, shutting down, or not completing startup, address the power or system issue before focusing on the printer. Expected outcome: The scanner is operating normally except for the printer function.

steps.title: Inspect the Printer Paper Roll
steps.instructions: Open the printer compartment and verify that thermal paper is installed, not depleted, and not damaged. Confirm the paper is not wrinkled, crushed, wet, contaminated, or jammed in the printer path. Expected outcome: Good thermal paper is present and able to feed freely. If replacing or reseating the paper resolves the issue, return the device to service after confirming proper print function.

steps.title: Verify Thermal Paper Orientation
steps.instructions: Confirm the thermal paper is loaded in the correct direction. Thermal printers only print on the coated side of the paper. A reversed roll may feed normally but produce a blank printout. Expected outcome: Paper feeds with the printable thermal side facing the print head as intended. If correcting the paper orientation restores printing, stop and document the resolution.

steps.title: Check the Printer Door or Cover
steps.instructions: Inspect the printer cover for proper closure, latch engagement, cracks, or obstruction. A partially open cover can prevent paper feed, cause poor print quality, or trigger printer errors. Expected outcome: The printer cover closes securely and the paper exits cleanly.

steps.title: Clear Any Visible Paper Jam
steps.instructions: Remove loose or jammed paper from the printer path without using sharp tools or forcing the mechanism. Do not pull aggressively against the print roller or internal printer assembly. Expected outcome: Paper path is clear and the roll advances normally. If a jam was cleared, perform a test print to verify normal operation.

steps.title: Inspect the Paper Feed Path
steps.instructions: Confirm the paper roll spins freely and is not installed too tightly in the compartment. Make sure the paper exits through the slot without binding or folding under the cover. Expected outcome: The printer can advance paper smoothly.

steps.title: Attempt a Test Print or Report Print
steps.instructions: From the appropriate print/report function, attempt to print a result or test output. Observe whether the printer: Feeds paper; Prints faintly; Prints blank; Stops immediately; Makes abnormal noise; Displays an error message. Expected outcome: The printer behavior is confirmed during an actual print attempt.

steps.title: Check for Low Battery or Power-Related Behavior
steps.instructions: If the printer fails only when the battery is low, connect the device to AC power or docking power if available and allow it to charge. Some devices may limit peripheral functions when power is insufficient. Expected outcome: Printer operation is tested under stable power conditions.

steps.title: Inspect for Contamination or Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Look for spilled gel, cleaner residue, paper dust buildup, cracked plastic, broken hinges, or damage around the printer area. Do not apply liquid cleaner directly into the printer mechanism. Expected outcome: No visible contamination or physical damage is preventing printer operation.

steps.title: Verify Print Settings or Workflow
steps.instructions: Confirm staff are using the correct print workflow and that the device is not set to store, save, or display results without printing. If available, check whether the result was captured properly before attempting to print. Expected outcome: The printer failure is not caused by workflow or setup confusion.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Turn the Portascan 3D off, wait briefly, then restart it. After startup, attempt another print using known-good paper. Expected outcome: Temporary software or peripheral communication issues are cleared. If printing resumes normally, document the power cycle and successful test print.

steps.title: Compare With Known-Good Paper
steps.instructions: If available, test with a known-good roll of compatible thermal printer paper. Poor-quality, aged, or incorrect paper can cause blank, faint, or inconsistent printouts. Expected outcome: Paper compatibility or paper condition is ruled out.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Failure Is Internal
steps.instructions: If the printer still does not feed, print, latch properly, or communicate after paper, power, cover, jam, and workflow checks are complete, the issue is likely internal to the printer mechanism, printer cable, control board, or related electronics. Expected outcome: External causes have been ruled out and the unit is ready for repair evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that the Laborie Portascan 3D would feed paper but produced a blank thermal printout after bladder scan exams.
CCR.Cause: Thermal paper roll was installed backwards, preventing the printer from marking the coated side of the paper.
CCR.Resolution: Reinstalled the thermal paper in the correct orientation, performed a successful test print, and returned the bladder scanner to service.

helpfulDetails: Whether the scanner powered on normally
Whether paper fed during print attempt
Whether printout was blank, faint, or incomplete
Signs of gel, cleaner residue, or paper dust

title: Laborie Portascan 3D Bladder Scanner - LCD touchscreen/display failure
description: Troubleshooting Portascan 3D display, touchscreen, dim screen, frozen screen, or unresponsive interface issues from external causes.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the Portascan 3D is not being used for an active patient assessment. If the scanner display or touchscreen is unreliable during patient care, clinical staff should stop using the unit and obtain another bladder scanner. Expected outcome: Patient care is not delayed or based on an unreliable device.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Display or Touchscreen Failure
steps.instructions: Ask staff what they observed. Common reports may include: Blank LCD screen; Dim or flickering display; Touchscreen does not respond; Screen freezes during scan; Incorrect or delayed touch response; Display works intermittently; Unit powers on but no usable image or menu appears. Expected outcome: The specific failure mode is confirmed before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Basic Power Status
steps.instructions: Verify the unit powers on normally. Confirm whether any power indicator, startup tone, backlight, logo screen, or boot activity appears. Expected outcome: The scanner shows signs of power. If there is no power at all, troubleshoot the issue as a power or battery problem instead of a display-only failure.

steps.title: Check Battery Charge Level
steps.instructions: Place the unit on its charger or connect the approved power supply, if available. Allow the scanner to charge long enough to rule out a deeply discharged battery. Expected outcome: A low-battery or power-related screen issue improves after charging. If the display returns to normal, document the low battery condition and stop.

steps.title: Verify Charger and Power Adapter Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect the charger, power adapter, docking contacts, and power cord for looseness, damage, bent pins, contamination, or poor seating. Try a known-good approved charger or power supply if available. Expected outcome: The display operates normally when reliable charging or external power is restored. If resolved, remove any defective charger or accessory from service.

steps.title: Inspect the Display Surface
steps.instructions: Check the LCD and touchscreen surface for cracks, fluid intrusion, pressure damage, delamination, dead areas, or signs the screen has been struck. Do not continue using the device if the display is cracked or unreadable. Expected outcome: Physical damage is identified or ruled out. A cracked or fluid-damaged display should be escalated for repair.

steps.title: Clean the Touchscreen Surface
steps.instructions: Clean the screen using facility-approved wipes compatible with the device. Remove gel residue, disinfectant film, glove powder, tape residue, or dried fluid that may interfere with touch response. Avoid excessive liquid near screen edges. Expected outcome: Touch response improves after the surface is cleaned. If resolved, return the scanner to service after functional verification.

steps.title: Check for Protective Film or Cover Interference
steps.instructions: Inspect for a lifting screen protector, wrinkled film, aftermarket cover, or debris trapped along the screen edge. Remove only non-required external coverings that are interfering with touch function, according to facility practice. Expected outcome: Touch input becomes accurate and responsive after the obstruction is removed.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Scanner
steps.instructions: Turn the Portascan 3D off, wait briefly, and power it back on. Observe whether the unit boots normally and whether the touchscreen responds at the home screen or menu. Expected outcome: A temporary software or interface lockup clears after restart. If resolved, document the freeze and verify normal operation.

steps.title: Check for Button or Control Interaction
steps.instructions: If the unit has physical power or navigation controls, confirm they are not stuck, damaged, or being held down by a case, cart, cradle, or accessory. Expected outcome: The display and touchscreen are not being affected by a stuck control or external pressure.

steps.title: Remove External Accessories From the Equation
steps.instructions: If the scanner is mounted on a cart, dock, charger, or bracket, remove it and test the unit handheld if safe. Look for pressure points, poor docking alignment, or accessory contact with the screen or controls. Expected outcome: The scanner functions normally away from the accessory. If so, inspect or replace the accessory before returning the device to use.

steps.title: Check for Overheating or Environmental Exposure
steps.instructions: Inspect the unit for unusual heat, chemical odor, swelling, moisture, or evidence it was stored in an extreme environment. Do not continue powering the unit if overheating, swelling, smoke, or liquid intrusion is suspected. Expected outcome: Environmental or safety concerns are identified and the device is removed from service if needed.

steps.title: Confirm Display Brightness or Screen Timeout Settings
steps.instructions: If the screen is visible but dim, check whether brightness, power-save, or timeout settings are causing the display to appear failed. Expected outcome: The display becomes usable after settings are corrected. If resolved, educate staff and document the setting change.

steps.title: Perform a Basic Functional Check
steps.instructions: After any corrective action, verify that the scanner: Powers on normally; Display is readable; Touchscreen responds accurately; Menus can be selected; Scan workflow can be entered; Battery or charging status displays properly; No display or control fault returns. Expected outcome: The scanner passes a basic operational check before return to service.

steps.title: Do Not Attempt Internal Display Repair at the Bedside
steps.instructions: If the LCD remains blank, flickers, freezes repeatedly, has dead touch zones, or fails after known-good power and cleaning checks, stop troubleshooting. Expected outcome: The failure is treated as a likely internal display, touchscreen, connection, control board, or software issue requiring bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Laborie Portascan 3D touchscreen was not responding and the display froze during bladder scan setup.
CCR.Cause: Verified the scanner powered on, charger functioned normally, and the touchscreen remained unresponsive after cleaning and power cycling.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the Portascan 3D from service, labeled it Out of Service, and sent it for bench evaluation for suspected display/touchscreen assembly or internal control fault.

helpfulDetails: Display condition: blank, dim, flickering, frozen, cracked, or distorted
Touchscreen response: none, delayed, inaccurate, or intermittent
Protective film or cart interference checked
Any signs of liquid intrusion, heat, odor, swelling, or impact damage
Whether the issue occurred during startup, scanning, or menu navigation

title: Laborie Portascan 3D Bladder Scanner - Inaccurate volume / poor bladder targeting
description: Troubleshooting inaccurate bladder volume readings, poor targeting, weak image acquisition, or inconsistent scan results caused by external setup issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the Portascan 3D is not being actively used for a clinical decision that could affect immediate patient care. If the reported bladder volume seems clinically inconsistent, instruct staff to verify the result per facility policy before catheterization or treatment decisions are made. Expected outcome: Patient care is not based on a questionable scan result. If the issue occurs during active patient care, move to another verified bladder scanner before continuing equipment troubleshooting.

steps.title: Confirm the Reported Problem
steps.instructions: Ask staff what was observed. Common reports may include: Inaccurate bladder volume; Unexpectedly high or low reading; Poor bladder targeting; Bladder not centered on scan; Repeated failed scans; Inconsistent results between scans; Volume does not match catheter output; Difficulty locating the bladder. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified before assuming scanner malfunction.

steps.title: Verify Proper Patient and Scan Conditions
steps.instructions: Confirm the patient was positioned appropriately and that the scan was performed over the expected bladder location. Check whether the issue occurred on one patient, multiple patients, or only with difficult anatomy. Bladder scanners can be affected by body habitus, abdominal wounds, ascites, surgical dressings, pregnancy status, bladder shape, or incorrect probe placement. Expected outcome: The scanner issue is separated from patient-specific scanning difficulty. If repeat scanning on a test subject or known-good condition produces normal results, document the finding and stop.

steps.title: Check Probe Placement and Targeting Technique
steps.instructions: Review whether the probe was aimed correctly toward the bladder and held steady during acquisition. The Portascan 3D is designed for automatic 3D scanning with targeting features and real-time scanning modes, so poor alignment or movement during scanning can still affect volume reliability. Expected outcome: The bladder is centered or targeted correctly before the volume is accepted. If improved technique resolves the inaccurate reading, educate staff, document no equipment failure found, and stop.

steps.title: Inspect Ultrasound Gel Use
steps.instructions: Confirm that adequate ultrasound gel was used between the probe and the patient. Look for dry scanning, too little gel, air gaps, old gel residue, or contamination on the probe face. Ultrasound bladder scanners rely on transmitted and reflected ultrasound signals to differentiate the bladder from surrounding tissue and calculate bladder volume. Expected outcome: Proper acoustic coupling is restored. If applying fresh gel and rescanning resolves the issue, clean the probe, document the cause, and stop.

steps.title: Inspect the Probe Face
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the probe surface for cracks, dents, residue, dried gel, damaged lens material, or signs of impact. Do not use sharp tools or harsh chemicals on the probe face. Expected outcome: The probe surface is clean, smooth, and undamaged. If physical damage is found, remove the device from service and send it for repair evaluation.

steps.title: Check the Probe Cable and Connector
steps.instructions: Inspect the probe cable for cuts, kinks, crushed sections, strain near the connector, or intermittent connection. Confirm the probe connector is fully seated and secure. Expected outcome: The scanner has a stable probe connection with no obvious external damage. If moving the cable causes image dropouts, scan failures, or changing results, remove the device from service for repair.

steps.title: Clean the Probe and Scanner Surfaces
steps.instructions: Clean the probe according to facility-approved cleaning procedures. Remove gel buildup around the probe face, cable strain relief, and scanner contact areas. Expected outcome: Signal quality is not being affected by residue or contamination. If cleaning restores normal targeting and volume readings, document the corrective action and return the scanner to service.

steps.title: Verify the Correct Scan Mode or User Selection
steps.instructions: Confirm staff are using the appropriate scan function for bladder volume or post-void residual workflow. Check for incorrect menu selection, incomplete scan process, or saved result being confused with a new scan. The Portascan 3D supports bladder volume and PVR scanning workflows, so workflow confusion can appear as an inaccurate device reading. Expected outcome: The scan mode matches the clinical task. If incorrect operation caused the concern, provide user guidance, document no equipment failure found, and stop.

steps.title: Check Battery and Power Status
steps.instructions: Verify the scanner has adequate battery charge. Low or unstable power can contribute to interrupted scans, incomplete acquisition, or unreliable operation. Expected outcome: The scanner remains powered throughout scan acquisition and result display. If the issue only occurs on battery power, charge the unit fully and retest. If the problem persists, remove the device from service for battery or power evaluation.

steps.title: Perform a Repeat Scan Under Controlled Conditions
steps.instructions: With clinical approval, repeat scanning under stable conditions using proper gel, clean probe surface, correct placement, and steady probe handling. Compare repeated results for consistency rather than relying on a single questionable scan. Expected outcome: Repeated scans are reasonably consistent and the bladder target is identifiable. If the scanner remains inconsistent despite controlled technique, continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Compare Against Another Verified Scanner
steps.instructions: If available, compare the Portascan 3D result with another known-good bladder scanner under similar conditions. Do not treat minor differences as equipment failure; focus on major discrepancies, repeated targeting failure, or clinically unreasonable results. Expected outcome: The suspected scanner either performs similarly to a known-good device or shows repeatable abnormal behavior. If another scanner produces reliable targeting and the Portascan 3D does not, remove the Portascan 3D from service.

steps.title: Review Stored Results or Printouts
steps.instructions: Check whether the reported volume came from a current scan, stored result, or printed record. Confirm the result was not misread, assigned to the wrong patient, or confused with a previous scan. Expected outcome: Documentation and displayed results match the actual scan performed. If the problem was user workflow or record confusion, document the finding and stop.

steps.title: Check for Display or Touchscreen Issues
steps.instructions: Verify the screen clearly shows targeting information, bladder outline, scan status, and result values. Confirm the touchscreen responds correctly and does not select the wrong function. Expected outcome: The operator can clearly view and select the correct scan workflow. If display or touchscreen problems affect scan targeting or result interpretation, remove the device from service for repair.

steps.title: Evaluate for Repeated Error Behavior
steps.instructions: Ask staff whether any error messages, failed scan prompts, abnormal sounds, startup issues, or freezing occurred. Observe whether the scanner repeatedly fails to acquire a target under normal use. Expected outcome: Any repeatable scanner behavior is identified before escalation. If the scanner freezes, fails acquisition repeatedly, or cannot produce a stable result, remove it from service.

CCR.Complaint: Nursing reported the Laborie Portascan 3D was giving inconsistent bladder volume readings and was difficult to target during patient scans.
CCR.Cause: Clinical Engineering found dried gel buildup on the probe face and inconsistent probe positioning during repeat scan review.
CCR.Resolution: Probe was cleaned, proper gel use and targeting technique were verified, repeat scans were consistent, and the scanner was returned to service.
